en.name: Leuconostoc mesenteroides
en.description: The representative probiotic that led the initial fermentation of traditional kimchi, producing a large amount of organic acid to provide refreshing carbonation and taste. It exhibits remarkable effects in inhibiting harmful bacteria in the body and regulating immunity.
en.ingredients: K-fermentation environment (optimal pH 1.5~2.5%, low temperature fermentation 5~10°C)
steps: During the initial fermentation stage of kimchi, lactic acid is produced to lower the environmental pH to approximately 4.5.
Prevents harmful bacterial growth and releases Dextran, a prebiotic fiber substance that protects the intestinal wall.
The fermentation gases, specifically carbon dioxide, contribute to the kimchi's characteristic tangy freshness.
en.sasang_relation: Very beneficial for Soyang-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Supports intestinal tract movement and controls heat toxins for Soyang-type who have active digestion but excess internal heat, which easily causes dryness in the large intestine or constipation.
en.bio_synergy: Improves gut beneficial bacteria occupancy by over 40%, promoting the production of viewtarete (lactose-derived substances), and helping to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

en.name: Bacillus subtilis (Hay Bacillus)
en.description: A strain of gut microbiota with high activity of amylase and protease, which helps in the fermentation of traditional Korean kimchi and doenjang, significantly enhances the absorption rate of protein from soybean proteins in the human body.
en.ingredients: Soybean paste, cooked soybeans, optimal fermentation temperature (37-42°C)
steps: The beneficial gut bacteria that thrive in soybeans accelerate the breakdown of protein in human flesh.
During fermentation, a large amount of poly-gamma-glutamic acid (poly-Glu) is formed.
Enhanced digestive enzyme and fibrin solubilizing enzyme (Nattokinase) production due to the action of probiotics.
en.sasang_relation: Extremely beneficial for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Warms and revitalizes the spleen and stomach, and supports large intestine immunity for Soeum-type who experience cold stomach energy, indigestion, and frequent gas bloating.
en.bio_synergy: It promotes synthesis of natural organic acids and vitamin B groups from strong acid and bile in the stomach, without being destroyed by them.

en.name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae (Koji Yeast)
en.description: A representative ascomycete that oversees the fermentation of traditional sake and tonkotsu alcohol through traditional methods. It produces a wide variety of vitamins B groups and essential amino acids, as well as aroma organic compounds during the fermentation process.
en.ingredients: Traditional fermented rice cake, boiled rice balls, clear water, fermentation temperature (20-25°C)
steps: When Aspergillus is broken down by koji in the koji-covered mold, it converts to glucose and then ethanol through fermentation.
Synthesized with ethyl ester of traditional Korean liquor, which imparts a unique fruit fragrance and floral scent reminiscent of traditional Korean spirits.
Through fermentation and filtration with koji, it completes the flavor of sake and tonkatsu.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Taeeum-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Enhances peripheral circulation to aid in maintaining body temperature and stimulates appetite.
en.bio_synergy: Vitamin B1, B2, and B6 are highly abundant, helping to remove stress substances such as ketones from the body and promoting the production of cellular energy metabolism.

en.name: Aspergillus oryzae (Hwang-guk-gyun)
en.description: This traditional fermentation and soybean product converts mulberries into amino acids, enhancing the primary substance (myoferment). It activates prebiotics that aid digestion and create a pleasant taste.
en.ingredients: Kombucha, rice, optimal cultivation humidity and temperature (28-32°C)
steps: The fermentation starter is evenly distributed throughout the koji by misting with water.
The enzyme with strong amylase activity that rapidly breaks down starch into sugars at a high speed.
Enhances production of probiotics to prevent bacterial proliferation as a root cause.
en.sasang_relation: All Body Type Balance Possible
en.wellness_tip: Pre-digested food structure to facilitate early breakdown, thereby promoting smooth absorption in individuals with insufficient digestive enzymes or elderly patients.
en.bio_synergy: It modulates intestinal pH and provides a supportive effect on the inhibition of Helicobacter pylori, thereby promoting positive effects on protein hydrolysis by natural proteolysis peptides and organic acids.

en.name: Seaweed Oyster Soup (Maesaengi Gulguk)
en.description: A traditional Korean dish from the southern part of Jeollanam-do during winter, a nourishing soup made with tender sea urchin and jelly-like seafood broth from the clean South Sea, is used to replenish energy.
en.ingredients:
Korean Ginseng: 300 grams  
Goji Berries: 150 grams  
Celery Root: 20 grams  
Chopped Garlic: 10 grams  
Soy Sauce: 1 large tablespoon  
Olive Oil: 1 small tablespoon  
Fish Soup Base (Salmon or Dashi)
steps: Mae-sa-eun geum cheo-eu beop-chyeo sae-ni-nyang pyeong-gil-eul seorim-geureu kjeol-eo geum-i-mug-dan.
Stir-fries a marinated fish, rinsed and drained squid with minced garlic to infuse the sea breeze aroma.
Place the anchovy paste in hot water until it boils, then turn off the heat and add the marinate.
After thoroughly mixing and heating until it thickens into a porridge-like consistency, it is then served as a stewed soup.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Soyang-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The cool and refreshing properties of Maesaengi lower the internal heat of Soyang-type and calm the spirit, while the rich zinc content of oysters boosts vitality.
en.bio_synergy: The taurine and calcium/magnesium combination in the oyster's and shrimp's hemoglobin helps clean out blood vessels, enhancing heart and circulatory system immunity during winter.

en.name: Angelica Root (Danggui) Haemul Pajeon (Angelica Seafood Pancake)
en.description: This fragrant medicinal herb from the mountainous areas of Gangwon-do, paired with abundant oysters and clams, creates a crispy earthy wellness food.
en.ingredients: Five-Ingredient Ginseng Leaf 50g, Side Greens 150g, Oyster Extract 1 fish, Squid Tail 50g, Flour 1 cup, Crushed Wheat Flakes 2 large spoons, Egg 1, Salad Oil/Table Spread
steps: The lotus leaf of Rehmannia root is rinsed with flowing water, then sliced to resemble the length of a side dish.
Mix together ground wheat flour and rice flour with ice water, then add sesame seeds before kneading into a dough.
Place the pancake batter evenly over a mixture of grated lotus root and ginger leaves, then sprinkle with sesame oil.
Place eggs on top and let the bottom become crispy. Turn it over to coat with oil from the edge, then shake it like a jar.
en.sasang_relation: Optimal tonic for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The strong tonifying effect of the blood nourishing action of Angelica sinensis leaves and its warming nature helps to improve circulation in those prone to cold extremities due to anemia.
en.bio_synergy: The extract of Rehmannia glutinosa (Schrad.) Hong (Rehmannia root), which contains the Decursin component, has remarkable effects in preventing aging and purifying blood circulation.

en.name: Bangpungnamul Haedok Muchim (Wind-dispelling Herb Salad)
en.description: This spring tonic medicinal food, made by blending the leaves of a wind-protecting herb to which the wind has been blocked, is seasoned with fermented soybean paste and wrapped in bamboo leaves.
en.ingredients: Green Tea Shoots 200g, Traditional Soy Sauce 1.5 large tablespoons, Hawthorn Berry 1 large tablespoon, Finely Chopped Garlic 1 small tablespoon, Sesame Oil 1 large tablespoon, Stir-Fried Bean Curd 1 small tablespoon
steps: Fungus-like windproof vegetable is trimmed of its rough edges and seasoned with a little salt before being boiled in boiling water for 2 minutes.
Dried and soaked in hot water until it is thoroughly moistened; then sliced to a size suitable for eating.
Mix traditional soybean paste with melon juice and finely chopped garlic to make a marinade.
It is stir-fried with radish sprouts and soy sauce, then sprinkled with sesame oil and crushed tofu.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Taeeum-type and Taeyang-type
en.wellness_tip: Prevents mucus in the throat due to poor breathing caused by dust and haze, and prevents inflammation of the respiratory organs.
en.bio_synergy: The coumarin content in Bangpungnamul enhances viral resistance in respiratory mucosa and aids anti-coagulation.

en.name: Nokdu Bindaetteok (Mung Bean Pancake)
en.description: This is a traditional Korean dish from the Gangwon Province that helps with digestion and energy enhancement by combining fermented soybeans, pork belly, water chestnuts, and shrimp in a spicy stir-fry with sesame oil and peanut sauce.
en.ingredients: Green Tea 2 cups, Millet Porridge 2 large servings, Pork Belly 100g, Napa Cabbage Kimchi 80g, Radish Greens 80g, Shredded Garlic 1 large serving, Sesame Oil 1 large serving, Sesame Oil
steps: Green tea and millet are rinsed, then soaked in water for 4 hours before being blended with a little water.
Steamed rice is shredded and then finely chopped, while shredded cabbage kimchi has its core removed to be thinly sliced.
Mixed with ground pork, kimchi, and pickled garlic, it is served in a spicy sauce.
Mix the seasoned filling with the fried green beans paste, then spread it evenly on a pan coated generously in oil.
en.sasang_relation: Particularly beneficial for Soyang-type
en.wellness_tip: The properties are strong, and its diuretic effect is remarkable. It assists in regulating the qi of the heart and lungs while facilitating urine excretion.
en.bio_synergy: The high content of flavonoids (vitexin, isovitexin) in mung beans inhibits free radicals and induces anti-inflammatory synergies.

en.name: Gilgyeong Licorice Root (Gamcho) Dorajitang
en.description: This is a traditional folk remedy for respiratory diseases, especially dry coughs. It relieves the bitter taste of bellflower root and soothes the mind and body.
en.ingredients: 30g of dried bellflower root, 10g of licorice, 5 jujubes, clean water
steps: Rinse the bellflower root in water or peel it and slice it thinly. Rinse it with clean water.
Add 1L of water to a pot, then add the bellflower root, licorice, and jujube. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour until the water is reduced by half.
Strain the soup through a fine cloth or cheesecloth into a bowl. Serve warm once per day in three servings.
en.sasang_relation: Excellent respiratory medicine for Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: This remedy helps protect and soothe the breathing passages of individuals with weak lung qi, especially during transitional seasons when they are prone to colds, asthma, or shortness of breath.
en.bio_synergy: The combination of platycodin sapogenin from bellflower root and glycyrrhizin from licorice promotes mucus secretion in the respiratory tract and calms the phlegm-producing center.

en.name: Yean-Yi Clan Soy-sauce Suyuk
en.description: This is a premium Korean dish that uses traditional 'sui-ganjang' from the Yean-Yi clan, which has been passed down for over 100 years. The dish features pork cooked in this aged 'sui-ganjang', adding authentic flavor without removing the natural juiciness of the meat.
en.ingredients: Pork belly/shoulder 1kg, aged 'sui-ganjang' 1/2 cup, 1 large bunch of scallions, ginger 1 tablespoon, jujube 5 pieces, licorice 2 slices, rice wine 3 tablespoons
steps: Prepare the pork by soaking it in hot water to remove excess fat and then cut into chunks.
Heat a pot with plenty of water, add 'sui-ganjang', scallions, ginger, jujube, and licorice. Simmer for about 10 minutes before adding the meat.
Add the pork when it's boiling; cook over low heat until tender, then cover and simmer on low heat for another 40 minutes.
When the pork is shredded easily with a fork, remove it from the pot and slice thinly.
en.sasang_relation: Tonic for Taeeum-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The deep 'sui-ganjang' fermentation amino acids in the Yean-Yi clan's aged soy sauce help enhance protein digestion, aiding in the absorption of proteins for both weak Yin types and strong Yang types.
en.bio_synergy: The rich melatonin content from fermented soy sauce has been shown to prevent fat oxidation in pork fats while regulating insulin sensitivity.

en.name: Jinju-Ha Clan Heotjesabab
en.english_name: Jinju-Ha Clan Hot Pot Rice Bowl
en.description: This is a hot pot rice bowl that Jinju-Ha Clan members eat without performing sacrificial rites. It consists of vegetables such as Chinese cabbage, bellflower root, and tofu, along with meat and fish, served over a savory broth made with soy sauce seasoning.
ingredients: 1 fish belly cooked in salted vinegar
5 large spoons of Tangkuk soup base
2 large spoons of Jeotjeol soy sauce seasoning
1 large spoon of fermented black bean
steps: Prepare the vegetables without using garlic and oil, only with salt and Jeotjeol soy sauce.
Add a little Tangkuk soup base to the cooked rice to make it soft.
Place the prepared rice in a bowl, add the three vegetables and seasoned fish belly, then garnish with sesame seeds.
Mix the fermented black bean seasoning without oil, add sesame seeds, and pour over the rice.
en.sasang_relation: Excellent for Soyang-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Avoid spicy seasonings such as garlic and chili peppers to soothe your stomach and prevent acid reflux in Lesser Yang Type individuals.
en.bio_synergy: The natural fiber of vegetables and high-quality protein from fish belly combine to increase the time for bowel movements, preventing sudden blood sugar spikes.

en.name: Gyeongju Choi-rich Clan Yukgaejang
en.description: This is a traditional Korean dish that was prepared for important guests or during times of famine, where the family would simmer pork and radish to share with neighbors.
en.ingredients: 400g lean pork belly, 3 large onions, 100g ginger, 100g tangerine peel, soy sauce 2 tablespoons, garlic oil 2 tablespoons, finely chopped green onion 1.5 tablespoons, fermented black beans
steps: Cook the lean pork belly in a pot with water until it is tender and then shred it.
Cut the ginger and tangerine peel into small pieces for seasoning before marinating them in soy sauce, garlic oil, and fermented black beans.
Add the seasoned vegetables to the simmering broth and cook for 30 minutes over low heat.
Chop the onions finely and add them to the soup along with garlic oil and fermented black beans. Simmer until it thickens slightly.
en.sasang_relation: Powerful energy boost for Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: This dish helps strengthen weak heart energy, particularly for those with a large frame who have weak blood circulation.
en.bio_synergy: The high-quality protein from pork belly and potassium from tangerine peel help improve kidney function and support cardiac muscle contraction.

en.name: Cheongsong Shim Clan Chicken Bulgogi
en.description: This is a unique traditional meat dish from the Cheongsong Shim Clan, simmered in water sourced from a mountain stream. The tender chicken heart and breast are finely chopped and cooked to perfection over an iron pot.
en.ingredients: 400g of chicken hearts and breasts, 2 large tablespoons of soy sauce, 1 large tablespoon of rice wine, 2 large tablespoons of diced garlic, 1 small tablespoon of ginger juice extract, 1 large tablespoon of honey, 2 large tablespoons of glutinous rice flour
steps: Remove the bones from the chicken and finely chop it until there are no more cracks.
Mix soy sauce, diced garlic, ginger juice extract, honey, rice wine, and glutinous rice flour into the chopped chicken to make a smooth batter.
Shape the batter into thin, round patties and place them on an iron pot or frying pan.
Cook over low heat until the oil is absorbed and the edges do not burn.
en.sasang_relation: Extremely beneficial for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The gentle nature of chicken meat helps warm the body, protect the intestinal wall, and enhance digestion in individuals with a sensitive constitution.
en.bio_synergy: The combination of unsaturated fatty acids from chicken meat and ginger juice extract's anti-inflammatory components reduces inflammatory cytokines in the body, supporting joint health.

en.name: Maekmundong Doenjang Paste (Liriope Tuber Bean Paste)
en.description: This Korean spicy bean paste soup is a blend of traditional fermented soybean paste and doenjang, enhanced with medicinal ingredients such as maekmundong (麦门冬), to create a base broth that reduces the acidity level during cooking while enhancing respiratory health.
steps: Mix the traditional fermented soybean paste into a bowl and gently knead to ensure even distribution.
Add finely ground maekmundong powder and coarsely ground buckwheat flour, then mix well. This helps neutralize the spiciness of the doenjang while enhancing its sweetness and richness.
Add honey to add moisture and control the amount of spices.
Pour into a jar or sealed container and let it ferment at room temperature for 2 weeks in an environment with low heat. Use this as the base broth for soups.
en.sasang_relation: Most ideal for Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: This doenjang paste soup is particularly beneficial for individuals with Greater Yin Type, enhancing their immune system and respiratory health by strengthening their defenses against dry lungs and frequent coughing during autumn or winter.
en.bio_synergy: Maekmundong saponin (opiporogin) remains intact even after heating in cooking, stimulating the secretion of mucus in the bronchial glands and alleviating symptoms of spasmodic coughs.

en.name: Sansa Maesil Cho-Chili Paste (Gochujang) (Hawthorn Plum Sweet Chili Paste)
en.description: This premium functional soy sauce is made by blending traditional Korean chili paste with dried mountain yam, honey, and natural sweet tea. It helps digest meat and fish while enhancing the flavor.
en.ingredients: Traditional chili paste 4 tablespoons, maesil (dried mountain yam) powder 2 tablespoons, honey 2 tablespoons, natural sweet tea 1.5 tablespoons, minced garlic 1 tablespoon, honey 1 tablespoon
steps: Place 20g of dried mountain yam in water until the water reduces to half its volume.
Mix with chili paste, maesil powder, and natural sweet tea, then stir until it thickens.
Add minced garlic and honey, then let sit at room temperature for one day before using.
en.sasang_relation: Digestive tonic for Soyang-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: This is a perfect dish to aid in digestion for those with excessive heat in their bowels or digestive issues, as well as regulating appetite.
en.bio_synergy: The organic acids (rutin and malic acid) from the mountain yam leaves and the pectin content of maesil help detoxify the liver and inhibit harmful bacteria such as food poisoning pathogens.

en.name: Samultang Salted Fish Dressing
en.description: Enriches circulation and blood nourishment with Angelica Root, Thousand Gold, Mugwort, and Mountain Yam. A special seasoning for Samulnotto.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons minced fish paste (eel paste), 2 tablespoons eel broth, 2 tablespoons Samulnotta dressing liquid, 1 tablespoon chopped green onion, 1 tablespoon ground ginger, 1 tablespoon chili pepper powder
steps: Prepare the Samulnotta seasoning with Angelica Root, Thousand Gold, Mugwort, and Mountain Yam. Mix minced fish paste (eel paste), eel broth, Samulnotta dressing liquid, chopped green onion, ground ginger, and chili pepper powder.
Mix minced garlic, shallots, green onions, and chili peppers to enhance the aroma of the seasoning.
en.sasang_relation: Specialized remedy for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Enhances circulation and blood nourishment for individuals with stagnant blood flow. Reduces cold hands and feet in individuals with pale skin.
en.bio_synergy: Samulnotta's medicinal ingredients interact with the tyrosine in the seasoning, promoting red blood cell production and preventing anemia in women by improving uterine blood circulation.

en.name: Maekmundong Gugija Herbal Wine
en.description: This traditional recipe combines the medicinal properties of Maekmundong (Liriope Tuber), Guji (Goji Berry), and Daqiu (Jujube) with the beneficial effects of Ginseng, Radix Isatidis (Mountain Yam), and Licorice to create a harmonious blend that soothes and nourishes the lungs.
en.ingredients: 2kg glutinous rice, 400g traditional koji, 50g prepared Maekmundong, 50g prepared Guji, 30g Daqiu, 5g Radix Isatidis, 1L clean water
steps: Mix Maekmundong, Guji, Daqiu, and Radix Isatidis with the rice wine and simmer over low heat until a thick medicinal broth is formed.
Soak glutinous rice in cold water for several hours to soften it before steaming until fully cooked.
Combine the steamed glutinous rice, koji, and prepared Maekmundong, Guji, Daqiu, and Radix Isatidis into an earthenware jar and mix thoroughly.
Allow the mixture to ferment at 22-25°C for approximately 3 weeks before straining off the clear liquid layer.
en.sasang_relation: Medicinal liquor for Soyang-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The synergistic effects of Guji's strengthening of the kidney qi and Maekmundong's lung-soothing properties help balance the energy levels in Lesser Yang individuals, aiding in the regulation of Qi deficiency and blood stasis.
en.bio_synergy: The alcohol extraction process allows for the incorporation of beta-amyloid (Betaine) from Guji and rutin from Daqiu into the digestive system, promoting protection of liver cells and strengthening of capillary walls.

en.name: Bongnyeong Tonic Makgeolli
en.description: This is a white, smooth tonic sake that enhances circulation and energy flow while detoxifying body moisture and waste. It is made by mixing fermented rice with black fungus powder and traditional koji, then fermenting it in an earthenware jar.
en.ingredients: 3kg brown rice, 150g black fungus powder, 600g traditional koji, clean water
steps: Mix the brown rice into a ball, sprinkle black fungus powder evenly during kneading to allow for absorption of its components.
Knead until fully mixed with koji powder and then add it to an earthenware jar.
Pour in 4L of clean water and mix thoroughly. Seal the jar opening with a cloth bag.
Let it ferment at room temperature (20-24°C) for 10 days, then strain out rice gruel and slightly dilute with water before drinking.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Taeeum-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Helps lymphatic circulation in individuals prone to lymph node congestion due to slow body moisture metabolism.
en.bio_synergy: The Pachyman (Pachyman) glycoconjugates of the black fungus powder and the beneficial by-products from the fermentation process synergistically enhance the function of the kidneys, promoting the elimination of waste products and stimulating the production of immune cells.

en.name: Ginseng Hwalhyeol Liquor (Ginseng Circulation Wine)
en.description: A premium Yakseon liquor made by combining high-quality Ginseng and Jujube, to promote blood circulation and enhance gut health. This winter remedy is specifically designed for those with Cold Hands & Feet.
en.ingredients: 6-year-old Ginseng root 2 roots, 20g of Kimchi seasoning, 10 large jujubes, 1 kg of millet rice porridge, 2L clear water, 300g salted soybean powder
steps: Clean and remove the brain from the Ginseng root.
Mix the sliced Ginseng root with seasoned Millet Rice Porridge, Jujube, Salted Soybean Powder, and Kimchi Seasoning in warm water.
Stir into a pot for 2 weeks at room temperature or slightly warmer (24-26°C) to allow fermentation.
en.sasang_relation: Irreplaceable special medicine for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: This is an exceptional remedy for those with low Yang Qi, experiencing cold hands and feet, poor digestion, and pale complexion due to insufficient blood flow.
en.bio_synergy: The Ginseng's Jinseonisoide (Rg1, Rb1, Rg3) components enhance the cell permeability of alcohol fermentation solution, thereby improving the speed of primary circulation.

en.name: Schisandra Berry (Omija) Sol잎 Control Sweet Makgeolli (Schisandra Berry (Omija) Pine-needle Sweet Makgeolli)
en.description: This sparkling traditional spumoni wine is made by combining five flavors of fermented glutinous rice with natural solvents to absorb the energy that dissipates, and adding chestnut leaves to smooth out the head. It is a healthy traditional sparkling wine.
en.ingredients: 2 kg glutinous rice, 80g Schisandra Berry (Omija) (Schisandra), 50g pine needles, 400g traditional koji, 3 L clear water
steps: Rinse and dry the chestnut leaves, then soak them in cold water overnight to extract the pigments.
Soak glutinous rice balls in hot water until they are soft. Add chestnut leaf juice, green tea, and koji powder to a bowl and mix thoroughly.
Mix the ingredients for 12 days at room temperature (20-22°C) in an airtight container.
After removing the sediment, cool it down by placing it in a refrigerator to maintain its refreshing quality.
en.sasang_relation: Excellent for Taeyang-type and Soyang-type
en.wellness_tip: It helps reduce excessive heat and energy flow upwards towards the upper body of people with an overactive Yang constitution, preventing their lower bodies from drying out easily and reducing irritability.
en.bio_synergy: The Schizandrin component in green tea and the terpenes component in chestnut leaves work together to regulate neurotransmitters in the brain and increase the production of enzymes that inhibit liver damage.

en.name: Traditional Soy-sauce Dallae Dressing
en.description: This springtime detox and blood circulation improvement sauce is made by infusing traditional Korean soy sauce with a spicy, warm spring season opener.
steps: Wash the dajeon thoroughly, removing its outer layer and dirt. Then slice it into thin strips.
Mix the aged soy sauce, ground pepper powder, sesame oil, and honey in a bowl.
Add the sliced daejeon to the mixture and stir well.
Just before serving, sprinkle with sesame oil and chopped green onions.
en.sasang_relation: Vitality boost for Soeum-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The spicy nature of the daejeon helps regulate gas in the digestive system, while the omega-3 fatty acids from sesame oil and amino acids from soy sauce help strengthen the qi of Lesser Yin Type individuals.
en.bio_synergy: The alliin compounds in dajeon interact with the peptides in traditional soy sauce to help stabilize blood pressure and improve the elasticity of small blood vessels.

en.name: Gamtae (Seaweed) Kimchi
en.description: This is a traditional seaweed kimchi made from the gamtae (seaweed), which is harvested in pristine coastal areas during winter. It has a strong marine aroma and enhances both strength and appetite.
en.ingredients: 300g of fresh gamtae, 100g of radish, 2 garlic cloves, 3 tablespoons of minced fish sauce, 1 tablespoon of finely chopped garlic, 1 small tablespoon of ginger, 2 tablespoons of ground red pepper, 1 cup of soybean paste
steps: Wash the gamtae thoroughly in salted water and rinse it several times until all the impurities are removed. Drain it well.
Chop the radish into thin strips, and mince the garlic cloves.
Mix minced fish sauce, ground red pepper, finely chopped garlic, and ginger with the kimchi seasoning.
Add the gamtae to the seasoned mixture, knead gently until it is evenly mixed without sticking together.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Soyang-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The minerals-rich gamtae helps lower the yang type's blood pressure, while aiding in the circulation of blood and energy flow in the weak yin body.
en.bio_synergy: The alginic acid fiber from the gamtae protects the intestinal mucosa, inhibits harmful bacterial absorption, and enhances the barrier protection effect of the gut by combining with probiotics.

en.name: Gat Mulkimchi (Leaf Mustard Water Kimchi)
en.description: A leaf mustard water kimchi made with red cabbage and spicy radish, featuring a beautiful autumn/winter color of the bright-colored soup broth and a fragrant, slightly pungent aroma.
en.ingredients: Red cabbage 500g, radish 300g, pear half, cilantro 50g, glutinous rice flour 3 tablespoons, garlic 4 cloves, ginger 1/2 tablespoon, salt 2.5 large spoons, water 8 cups
steps: Wash and chop the red cabbage into 4cm lengths, then slightly season with salt.
Chop the radish thinly and peel the pear before seasoning with salt.
Mix glutinous rice flour and salt in a pot to make kimchi broth. Add sliced garlic and ginger, then mix well.
Add seasoned red cabbage, cilantro, radish, and pear into the kimchi jar, and let it simmer at room temperature for 1 day before refrigerating.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Soeum-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: Red cabbage's warm nature protects the weak stomach of Lesser Yin Type, while promoting lung function in Greater Yin Type.
en.bio_synergy: The anthocyanins from red cabbage and fermented by-products promote the anti-oxidative activity of epithelial cells and induce the production of immune-active factors.

en.name: Gaeseong Bossam Kimchi
en.description: This is a traditional, high-quality royal kimchi from Gaeseong, featuring abundant ingredients such as napa cabbage leaves, garlic, sea cucumber, dried shiitake mushrooms, chestnuts, and pears. It's a special banquet condiment that enhances vitality.
en.ingredients: 1 large head of shredded napa cabbage, 500g of garlic, 1 whole dried shiitake mushroom, 2 medium-sized dried sea cucumber slices, 3 chestnuts, 1 tablespoon of pears, 50g of water dropwort, 50g of radish, 1 cup of ground red pepper flakes, soy sauce, and shrimp paste broth
steps: Cut off the outer leaves of the napa cabbage to remove any packaging. Slice the inner leaves.
Chop dried sea cucumber and shiitake mushrooms into small pieces. Thinly slice garlic, water dropwort, chestnuts, and pears.
Mix ground red pepper flakes, shrimp paste broth, garlic, water dropwort, and pears with the cabbage mixture.
Place 3-4 layers of shredded cabbage leaves on a plate, pour in the seasoned cabbage mixture, then roll up tightly. Place in an earthenware jar to steam until cooked.
en.sasang_relation: Balanced
en.wellness_tip: The abundant taurine and amino acids from sea cucumber and dried shiitake mushrooms help strengthen the qi of individuals prone to weakness due to seasonal changes.
en.bio_synergy: The rich nucleotides and essential amino acids in seafood promote the proliferation of beneficial gut flora, increasing the diversity index (Diversity Index) of intestinal microorganisms.

en.name: Gegukji Kimchi
en.description: This is a traditional Korean side dish, known as the representative seafood kimchi of the eastern coastal region of North Chungcheong Province. It is made by adding small fish (like eels) or dried fish broth to seasoned and mashed napa cabbage, radish, garlic, and mountain yam soup.
en.ingredients: 600g of pickled mustard greens, 300g of aged turnip, 200g of radish, 1 cup of dried fish broth, 3 tablespoons of ground ginger, 2 tablespoons of minced garlic, 2 tablespoons of mountain yam juice
steps: Cut the pickled mustard greens and radish into small pieces, then mash the aged turnip.
Prepare the dried fish broth by peeling and finely chopping the eel or small fish.
Mix in the chopped eel or small fish broth with the seasoned and mashed napa cabbage, radish, garlic, mountain yam soup, ground ginger, minced garlic, and mountain yam juice.
Pack into a kimchi jar and let it ferment for 3-4 days before simmering in a pot to make a stew-like dish.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Taeeum-type and Soyang-type
en.wellness_tip: The detoxifying properties of aged turnip and the lubricating effect on the Great Yin type, as well as the softening effect on the lesser yang type.
en.bio_synergy: During fermentation, the toluol glucosides in eel skin or small fish skin combine with the metabolic products of beneficial bacteria in the gut, enhancing the activity of immune cells.

en.name: Gogumajulgi Kimchi (Sweet Potato Stem Kimchi)
en.description: This is a summer specialty Korean side dish, made to be eaten when there's not enough napa cabbage and the weather gets hot. It has sweet potatoes with tender skin that are stuffed with radishes and seasoned with spicy seasonings.
en.ingredients: 500g sweet potato stems, 100g green onions, 4 tbsp soy sauce, 3 tbsp minced garlic, 1.5 tbsp chopped green onion, 1 small cup ginger, 2 tbsp sesame oil, salt
steps: Peel and slice the sweet potato stems into 5cm lengths, then rinse them in cold water to remove excess starch.
Wash the green onions thoroughly and chop them into 4cm lengths.
Mix minced garlic, ginger, green onion, sesame oil, and soy sauce for a spicy seasoning.
Combine the seasoned seasonings with shredded sweet potato stems and green onions. Mix well and let it sit in the refrigerator for one day to cool down before serving.
en.sasang_relation: Benefits Soyang-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The combination of high fiber content from sweet potato stems and warm properties from green onions helps prevent constipation in individuals with Yin deficiency, promoting healthy digestion.
en.bio_synergy: The non-sticky cell wall fibers found in the sweet potato stem cells create a great environment for beneficial bacteria (prebiotics) to thrive, which is excellent for gut health.

en.name: Godeulppaegi Kimchi (Korean Lettuce Kimchi)
en.description: This is a premium winter kimchi from the southern region, featuring a unique aroma of lesser yang type and spicy taste. It is seasoned with garlic, radish, and other seasonings.
en.ingredients: 1 kg Korean lettuce, 100 g cilantro, 1 cup soy sauce, 1/2 cup anchovy paste, 2 tablespoons sesame oil, 3 tablespoons chestnut powder, 1/2 cup diced garlic, 2 tablespoons minced radish, 2 tablespoons minced green onion, 1/2 cup chopped scallions, 2 tablespoons minced garlic, 1/4 cup ground pepper
steps: Wash the Korean lettuce thoroughly and cut it into small pieces. Soak in salt water for 3-5 days until the bitterness is reduced.
Mix soy sauce, anchovy paste, garlic, scallions, and pepper to make a flavorful seasoning.
Pour the seasoned mixture over the dried Korean lettuce and cilantro, then mix well from top to bottom.
Store in a kimchi jar for 3-4 days at room temperature until it is soft and crisp. Store in a cool place after that.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Soyang-type
en.wellness_tip: The spicy taste of the Korean lettuce helps to relieve stress by clearing away heat from the heart and lungs.
en.bio_synergy: Inulin, a natural polysaccharide found in the root, helps regulate blood sugar levels and strengthens intestinal walls, aiding in the relief of chronic digestive symptoms.

en.name: Gugangtae Kimchi (Gangtai Seaweed Kimchi)
en.description: This unique seaweed kimchi is a winter specialty from the coastal areas of Quancheng, Gwangju. It is made by gently mixing tender sea lettuce with minced anchovy paste and soy sauce seasoning.
en.ingredients: sea lettuce 400g, 2 large spoons of anchovy paste, 1 large spoon of soy sauce, 2 large spoons of chopped garlic, 1 large spoon of ginger, salt
steps: Wash the sea lettuce in warm water to remove sand and debris. Drain it well and pat dry with paper towels.
Mix soy sauce, anchovy paste, minced garlic, chopped green onions, and ground pepper into a seasoning paste.
Gently tear open the drained sea lettuce and mix it into the seasoning paste.
Let sit at room temperature for two days until the kimchi is soft and has developed a subtle aroma of seaweed and unique sea vegetable flavors.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial to Soeum-type and Taeeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The high iodine and calcium content in the tender sea lettuce helps protect the mucous membranes of the lower digestive tract for Lesser Yin Type individuals, while cleansing cholesterol levels and preventing heart disease for Greater Yin Type individuals.
en.bio_synergy: Seaweeds' carotenoids and anchovy fermentation acids regulate the pH balance of gut bacteria, inhibiting the growth of pathogenic harmful bacteria such as well-organized beneficial bacteria.

en.name: Perilla Leaf Kimchi
en.description: This summer kimchi is a refreshing, natural anti-inflammatory herb called perilla leaf cooked in salt water. It's then seasoned with various vegetables such as daikon, water dropwort, chestnut, garlic, and ginger to make it delicious.
en.ingredients: Perilla leaves 100 pieces, daikon 30g, radish 20g, chestnuts 2 pieces, ground pepper powder 4 tablespoons, soy sauce 2 tablespoons, sesame oil 2 tablespoons, minced garlic 1 tablespoon, honey 1 tablespoon
steps: Wash perilla leaves in running water and remove the stems. Drain them well.
Shred daikon, radish, chestnuts into very fine pieces.
Mix ground pepper powder, soy sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, and shredded vegetables to make seasoning.
Fold 2 perilla leaves together and spread a little of the seasoning on top. Gradually add more seasoning until it's evenly distributed. Place in an airtight container for two days to ferment.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The warm, fragrant perilla leaf characteristic phenylethanol content helps to soothe the stomach and improve digestion in individuals with insufficient qi.
en.bio_synergy: It also contains rosmarinic acid, a type of antioxidant polyphenol that inhibits inflammation-causing cytokines in the intestinal lining, helping to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

en.category: Kkolttugi Mu Kimchi (Baby Octopus Radish Kimchi)
en.name: Kkolttugi Mu Kimchi
en.description: A savory and tangy radish kimchi made by mixing fresh octopus, crispy radishes, seasonings, and teas.
en.ingredients: 300g baby octopus, 500g radish, 40g waterwort, 5 large spoons of soy sauce, 3 large spoons of fish lees, 1.5 large spoons of minced garlic, 1 small spoon of ginger, 2 large spoons of kimchi lees, 1 large pot
steps: Clean and rinse the octopus with salt water until it's clean, then soak in soy sauce.
Cut the radish into thick, long strips and marinate in salt until it's fully drained.
Mix seasonings such as soy sauce, fish lees, garlic, ginger, and kimchi lees, then add the marinated octopus and radish to a pot.
Add waterwort and mix well. Cook on low heat for 5-7 days until crispy and spicy.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Taeeum-type and Soeum-type
en.wellness_tip: The abundant tyramine and digestive enzymes in octopus, combined with the beneficial enzymes in radish, help improve blood circulation and digestion in individuals with weak heart function.
en.bio_synergy: The digestible proteins in octopus are broken down into natural active peptides by gut bacteria, leading to a synergistic effect on blood pressure regulation and protection of blood vessel walls.

en.name: Kkwong Kimchi (Pheasant Meat Kimchi)
en.description: This is a traditional Korean kimchi recipe from the ancient book 'Eumsikdimibang' that has been preserved, featuring a meat broth made by cooking pheasant meat in clear broth and then simmering it with oysters, radishes, and ginger. It was originally served as a winter imperial dish.
en.ingredients: 1/2 kilogram of pheasant meat, 2 oysters, 300 grams of radish, 30 grams of waterwort, 2 tablespoons of salt, 1 small tablespoon of chopped fresh ginger, 1 large clove garlic, 1 large onion, 1 large carrot, 1 teaspoon of sesame seeds
steps: Cook the pheasant meat in a pot with bones to remove excess fat. Remove the fat and chop it into thin strips.
Cut oysters and radishes into coin-shaped pieces, sprinkle salt on them lightly, then rinse off the water.
Mix cooked pheasant broth with chopped ginger and oyster juice, then simmer for 2-3 days until soft and cold.
Add chopped oysters, radish, and shredded pheasant meat to a kimchi pot and cook over low heat for 1 hour. Serve chilled.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Lesser Yin Type (Soojung) and Greater Yin Type (Taejung)
en.wellness_tip: This pheasant meat kimchi is beneficial for those with a high Yang Qi and low fat content, helping to clear the lower abdomen of Lesser Yin Type individuals while strengthening their vitality.
en.bio_synergy: The combination of animal-derived low-fat collagen and lactobacillus fermentation products helps repair the tight junctions in the intestinal epithelium (Tight Junction) and supports the synthesis of immune globulin.

en.name: Aged Pumpkin Kimchi
en.description: This is a winter delicacy kimchi made with well-seasoned, aged pumpkin. It's seasoned with garlic, ginger, and other seasonings.
en.ingredients: 1 kg of aged pumpkin, 500g of napa cabbage sprouts, 300g of green onion scallion, 1 cup of ground pepper, 1/2 cup of fermented fish sauce, 3 tablespoons of minced garlic, 1 tablespoon of minced ginger, salt
steps: Peel and clean the pumpkin seeds, then remove the skin. Cut it into small pieces that are about as big as your thumb.
Wash and rinse the napa cabbage sprouts and green onion scallion with salt to remove excess water. Then cut them into 5 cm lengths.
Mix fermented fish sauce, ground pepper, minced garlic, and ginger together to make a seasoning paste.
Place the seasoned pumpkin and cabbage in a kimchi jar that has been sealed tightly. Cook it at low temperature for 2 weeks or more until it's fully cooked.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Lesser Yang Type (Soybean) and Greater Yin Type (Ginger) individuals
en.wellness_tip: The strong diuretic effect of aged pumpkin helps regulate the condition of Lesser Yang Type, while ginger in the spicy seasoning supports the relief of cold symptoms in Greater Yin Type.
en.bio_synergy: The carotenoids in the pumpkin combine with beneficial probiotics to induce vitamin A synthesis on the intestinal mucosa and enhance immune defense against cancer.

en.name: Wild Garlic Kimchi (Wild Garlic Kimchi)
en.description: This springtime kimchi is made with wild garlic, a spicy and fragrant wild garlic variety from Jeju dialect: 'Mugil Mankang'. It's seasoned with light and spicy seasonings to enhance immunity.
en.ingredients: 300g of wild garlic, 3 tablespoons of ground red pepper powder, 2 tablespoons of minced fish sauce, 1 tablespoon of pickled persimmon juice, 1/2 tablespoon of chopped garlic, 20 grams of fermented tofu
steps: Wash the wild garlic thoroughly and clean it in a bowl. Drain excess water.
Mix ground red pepper powder, fish sauce, pickled persimmon juice, and minced garlic to make a light and spicy seasoning.
Cut the wild garlic into bite-sized pieces and mix with the seasonings until well combined.
Serve immediately or heat through for 1 hour in an oven at 200°C (392°F) to enhance flavors.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Lesser Yin Type
en.wellness_tip: The unique warm and spicy taste of wild garlic, which is beneficial for those with a lesser yin constitution due to their weak digestive capacity, poor blood circulation, and tendency towards cold hands and feet.
en.bio_synergy: Wild garlic's allicin component helps regulate bloating in the stomach by inhibiting harmful gas-producing bacteria in the large intestine, supporting the growth of beneficial gut flora.

en.name: Meowi Kimchi (Butterbur Leaf Kimchi)
en.description: This traditional wellness kimchi is made from the slightly fried leaves of butterbur, a medicinal herb used as an herbal remedy. It helps regulate digestion and promotes heat production.
en.ingredients: 500g butterbur leaves, 50g garlic, 4 large spoons soy sauce, 3 large spoons fermented fish paste, 1 large spoon pickled green onion, 1.5 large spoons minced garlic, 3 large spoons fresh mint
steps: Wash the butterbur leaves gently and remove any stems; soak in salt water for one hour to reduce bitterness.
Mix together fermented fish paste, soy sauce, pickled green onions, and minced garlic. Season with fresh mint.
Place the drained butterbur leaves on top of the seasoned kimchi mixture, spreading it evenly.
Pack into a jar and let sit at room temperature for one hour before refrigerating to allow fermentation; serve as an accompaniment to meat dishes.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Lesser Yang Type (Sasang) and Greater Yin Type (Tasang)
en.wellness_tip: The bitter taste of butterbur helps regulate the yang qi in the lesser yang type, while it soothes the dry throat caused by the dryness of the greater yin type.
en.bio_synergy: The antioxidant polyphenols found in butterbur and the beneficial lactobacillus strains from fermentation help control inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.

en.name: Pungmaneul Kimchi (Green Garlic Kimchi)
en.description: This is a seasonal immune-enhancing kimchi that combines the spicy, earthy flavor of garlic and green onions with the savory tanginess of fermented fish broth and glutinous rice.
en.ingredients: 500g green onions, 5 large tablespoons soy sauce, 4 large tablespoons fermented fish broth, 3 large tablespoons glutinous rice flour, 1 small tablespoon grated ginger, 1.5 large tablespoons honey, 2 cups
steps: Cut the green onion roots and wash it clean with water until no dirt remains.
Soak in salted water for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Mix soy sauce, fermented fish broth, glutinous rice flour, grated ginger, honey, and 2 cups of water into a thick seasoning paste.
Add the seasoned green onion mixture to the soaked green onions and gently fold until no more liquid is visible. Let it sit for two days before eating.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Lesser Yin Type
en.wellness_tip: The strong digestive action of green onions helps stimulate the secretion of stomach acid in the case of individuals with a lesser yin constitution, improving digestion and strengthening qi.
en.bio_synergy: Rich natural anthocyanins perform probiotic roles to maximize the production of beneficial gut flora, thereby enhancing immunity.

en.name: Gaji Sobagi (Stuffed Eggplant Kimchi)
en.description: This is a summer kimchi dish from the eastern part of South Korea, featuring abundant anthocyanins in eggplants. It's made by stuffing them with garlic, radish, ginger, and other ingredients into a pot of broth or stew until it turns soft.
en.ingredients: 5 eggplants, 100g green onions, 100g radishes, 3 tablespoons ground pepper powder, 2 tablespoons minced garlic, 1 tablespoon minced green onion juice, 1 large cup water
steps: Peel and slice the eggplant into 6cm lengths. Remove both ends of each piece and make a deep crevice in the center with a knife.
Chop the green onions and radishes finely. Mix them with minced garlic, ground pepper powder, and minuscule amounts of ginger juice and salt.
Place the stuffed eggplant into a pot filled with broth or stew until it becomes soft.
Pour warm water over the top to cool down the temperature slightly.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Lesser Yang Type – Active & Energetic Body Type
en.wellness_tip: The combination of cold eggplant and hot ginger juice helps regulate heat in the upper body, alleviating symptoms such as chest discomfort or thirst.
en.bio_synergy: The nasin component found in eggplants enhances the growth of beneficial bacteria in the gut, reducing blood vessel oxidative stress and improving circulation.

en.name: Meongge Kimchi (Sea Squirt Kimchi)
en.description: This spicy and sweet traditional Korean sea vegetable kimchi is made by preserving the meaty, delicate taste of sea squirts with water dropwort, radish greens, garlic, ginger juice, and salt. It's a refreshing, slightly salty kimchi that can be eaten fresh within two to three days.
en.ingredients: 400g cleaned sea squirts, 50g water dropwort, 30g pickled mustard, 2 large spoons of fish sauce, 1 large spoon of minced garlic, 1 small spoon of ginger juice, 1 large spoon of minced garlic, 1 small spoon of ginger juice, 1 large spoon of sugar
steps: Wash the sea squirts thoroughly in cold water to remove excess salt and moisture.
Shred the water dropwort and pickled mustard into 3cm lengths.
Mix fish sauce, garlic, ginger juice, and minced garlic together for a tasty seasoning.
Add the shredded sea squirts first before mixing with the seasoned ingredients. Keep it tightly packed to prevent sticking and refrigerate until it's ready to eat within three days.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for both Lesser Yang Type (Soybean) and Greater Yin Type (Ginger) individuals
en.wellness_tip: The sea squirts' cool, soothing nature helps regulate the heat of the Lesser Yang type while preventing headaches and dry eyes. The abundant tryptophan in the sea squirts benefits the Greater Yin type by alleviating liver fatigue.
en.bio_synergy: The unique tryptophan and boron content in sea squirts combine with probiotics to stabilize blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity.

en.name: Myeongnan Radish Kimchi
en.description: This is a traditional Korean kimchi made with radishes, garlic, and ginger. It has been preserved in the warm climate of South Korea's eastern coast for over two years.
en.ingredients: Radish 500g, Garlic 150g, Mustard Greens 30g, Radish Sprouts 30g, Grated Ginger 3 tablespoons, Garlic Paste 1 tablespoon, Green Onion 1 tablespoon, Ginger Juice 1 small cup, Kimchi Seasonings
steps: Cut the radish into a slightly thick slice and marinate in salt for 20 minutes before washing and squeezing out excess water.
Grate garlic with a knife.
Mix grated garlic, ginger paste, green onion, and ginger juice to make a spicy kimchi seasoning.
Combine seasoned kimchi with boiled radish and mustard greens, then mix well until the radish is evenly distributed. Let it ferment for two weeks.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Lesser Yin Type and Greater Yin Type
en.wellness_tip: The rich vitamin B group in garlic and warm properties of radish's seasoning help to soften the stomach, improve digestion efficiency, and aid in recovery from fatigue.
en.bio_synergy: Protein is completely converted into amino acids during fermentation, which helps facilitate the absorption of nutrients by digestive organs and boosts the immune system of intestinal bacteria.

en.name: Banji Kimchi (Beef Stock Water Kimchi)
en.description: A high-quality specialty kimchi, a traditional Korean dish from the Geumgang region, made with beef broth water that is gently simmered to optimize gut health.
en.ingredients: 1 large napa cabbage, 300g garlic, 4 cups of beef stock water, 40g water dropwort, 1 large onion, 1 medium radish, 3 cloves garlic, 1/2 tablespoon ginger, salt
steps: Boil the beef broth until it's reduced to a smooth consistency. Strain and cool.
Cut the garlic into thin slices, peel the water dropwort, and slice the onion and radish into 4cm lengths. Cut the garlic and ginger into thin strips for storage.
Mix salt with the beef broth to create a tangy soup.
Place the cabbage, garlic, water dropwort, onion, radish, and ginger in a kimchi jar. Pour the prepared broth over it and let it ferment for two days.
en.sasang_relation: suitable for all constitutions (general)
en.wellness_tip: This is the safest type of kimchi for people who cannot eat spicy kimchi due to acid reflux or weakened stomach lining, as it eliminates excessive garlic flavor and combines the rich glutamic acid from beef broth with the beneficial properties of ginger.
en.bio_synergy: The amino acids in the beef stock water help promote the growth of Lactococcus (Leuconostoc) bacteria that produce lactic acid, strengthening cell wall protein bonds.

en.name: Seongnyu Kimchi
en.description: The pomegranate-shaped kimchi is made by rolling water dropwort into a neat shape, then filling it with radish, chestnut, and water dropwort. It is simmered in clear soup during the autumn/winter season for traditional imperial court kimchi.
en.ingredients: 2 unpeeled radishes, 40g water dropwort, 30g mung beans, 2 unpeeled chestnuts, 50g pomegranate, a little persimmon, 1 small cup of ginger juice, 2 large cups of salted water
steps: Peel the radishes and roll them into a neat shape. Cut the water dropwort, mung beans, chestnuts, and persimmons into thin slices and mix with ginger juice. Place the prepared ingredients inside the rolled radish.
Place the pomegranate-shaped kimchi in a pot filled with salted water and simmer for 3 days until it is cool enough to eat.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Lesser Yang Type (Soybean) and Greater Yin Type (Ginger) individuals
en.wellness_tip: The warm, fresh aroma of radish and water dropwort helps balance the qi of the lesser yang type and greater yin type organs by moistening the lungs and clearing phlegm in the chest.
en.bio_synergy: The abundant isothiocyanate content in pomegranate-shaped kimchi aids in detoxifying harmful substances in cells, especially those that are carcinogenic.

en.name: Songsongi (Royal Diced Radish) Kimchi
en.description: This royal diced radish kimchi is a traditional Korean dish that was prepared for the emperor's table. It has a smaller size than regular ones, which are easier to chew and have a spicy taste with clear broth.
en.ingredients: Radish 1kg, Water Dropwort 30g, Grated Ginger 3 tbsp, Fish Sauce 1.5 tbsp, Garlic Paste 1.5 tbsp, Minced Garlic 1 tbsp, Ginger Juice 1 small tbsp, Sugar 1 small tbsp, Salt
steps: Cut the radish into a size of 1.2cm and season with salt for 15 minutes until it is well seasoned.
Mix grated ginger with seasoned radish to make a bright color before adding fish sauce, minced garlic, ginger juice, shrimp paste, and shrimp broth.
Add shrimp paste, minced garlic, ginger juice, shrimp broth, and mince the sliced radish finely. Cook in a pot until it is well mixed for 1/2 hour, then cool down to eat cold.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Yin/Yang types
en.wellness_tip: This small size helps maximize contact area between digestive enzymes and the stomach acid of Yin/Yang types who have weak digestion or a tendency to feel bloated.
en.bio_synergy: The activity of amylase enzyme is enhanced by fermentation, shortening the time for carbohydrate digestion and contributing to blood sugar stability.

en.name: Suk Kkaktugi (Cooked Radish Kimchi)
en.description: This unique traditional kimchi is made by lightly sauteing radishes in a mixture of salt water and then shaping them into a soft, smooth texture. It is especially suitable for people with weak teeth or elderly individuals with poor intestinal health.
en.ingredients: 1 kg Radish, 30g Garlic, 3 tablespoons Salted Pepper, 1 tablespoon Minced Garlic, 1 tablespoon Minced Green Onion, 1 tablespoon Fermented Soybean Extract
steps: Cut the radishes into a size of 2cm in all directions and place them in boiling salt water for about 2 minutes until they turn translucent.
Mix the salted pepper thoroughly with the sautéed radishes to create a bright red color.
Combine the fermented soybean extract, garlic, green onion, and fennel seed into a seasoning paste. Mix it with the sautéed radishes.
Add the garlic powder and mix well before wrapping the radish in garlic powder. Afterward, heat the radish to room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Lesser Yin Type Individuals and Elderly People
en.wellness_tip: It can prevent excessive gas production due to weak intestinal health of those with a lesser yin type constitution. It also helps in softening the breakdown of carbohydrates.
en.bio_synergy: By heat treatment, we soften the rigid pectin bonds in radishes before they are fully oxidized, preventing irritation of the digestive tract and facilitating the absorption of beneficial probiotics.

en.name: Scale-cut Radish Kimchi
en.description: This traditional Korean kimchi is made by slicing the radish into scales and placing it in a small cavity of the fish roe, then filling with cabbage seasoning. The harmonious combination of the radish's sweetness and the cabbage's seasoning makes for an excellent dish.
en.ingredients: 2 large radishes, 200g sliced green cabbage, 40g water dropwort, 30g mung bean sprouts, 1/2 cup ground ginger powder, 3 tablespoons fish roe vinegar, 2 tablespoons ginger juice
steps: Wash the radish thoroughly and cut it into scales. Rinse with cold water to remove dirt, then slice into small pieces.
Slice the green cabbage thinly and mix with water dropwort, mung bean sprouts, ground ginger powder, fish roe vinegar, and ginger juice.
Fill each cavity of the sliced radish with the prepared cabbage seasoning.
Leave the kimchi in a jar until the radish's natural broth appears. Cook at low temperature for one week to cool down.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Yin/Yang types
en.wellness_tip: The refreshing and cold qi of the radish helps form a moisturizing fluid in the lungs of Yang types, preventing dry coughing during transitional seasons and promoting the expulsion of bodily waste.
en.bio_synergy: The combination of cabbage's and radish's complex cellulose fibers from the digestive tract wall can physically stimulate the release of fat-inducing intestinal bacteria, thereby inhibiting the growth of harmful gut flora.

en.name: Sunmu Baendaengi Kimchi
en.description: This is a large, spicy and flavorful bone-enhancing health kimchi made with the main ingredient of Korean radish called 'Baendaengi' from the coastal area of Gangwon-do. It also includes bone marrow broth that has been simmered to remove impurities.
en.ingredients: 1.5kg strong radishes, 200g radish leaves, 150g bone marrow broth, 3/4 cup ground ginger, 2 tablespoons minced garlic, 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger, 50g green onions, 50g chili pepper
steps: Cut the radishes into large pieces and peel them. Rinse the radish leaves thoroughly.
Prepare bone marrow broth by simmering it until it is thickened or use a knife to remove impurities from the bone marrow.
Mix the radishes, radish leaves, bone marrow broth, ground ginger, minced garlic, and fresh ginger into a bowl and knead vigorously.
Due to its natural properties, strong radishes are not required for this kimchi. It is simmered for 10 days until it becomes soft enough to be removed from the radish without breaking.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Lesser Yin Type – Thoughtful & Sensitive Body Type and Greater Yin Type – Strong & Enduring Body Type
en.wellness_tip: The unique spicy aroma of radishes and the strong taste of fresh ginger help to warm up the cold body type, while bone marrow broth helps strengthen bones without causing joint problems.
en.bio_synergy: The isothiocyanate component in radish and the peptidase content in bone marrow enhance the efficiency of detoxification (Phase II) by interacting with gut flora.

en.name: Deodeok Kimchi (Wild Codonopsis Kimchi)
en.description: This is a traditional Korean dish made by pounding the Deodeok root, then adding water dropwort and green onions to create a spicy and tangy seasoning. It is considered one of the best health supplements for those with weak constitutions.
en.ingredients: 400g Deodeok root, 50g water dropwort, 40g green onion, 4 tablespoons chili powder, 2 tablespoons minced garlic, 1 tablespoon grated ginger, 2 tablespoons glutinous rice flour
steps: Deodeok is soaked in salted water and then pounded to remove its natural flavor.
Water dropwort and green onions are cut into 4cm lengths.
Mix the minced garlic, ginger, green onion, and glutinous rice flour thoroughly to make a spicy kimchi seasoning.
Add the seasoning to the Deodeok root and mix well. Then add water dropwort, green onions, and grated garlic, and let it simmer at low heat for 2-3 days until it is flavorful.
en.sasang_relation: Strong & Enduring Body Type
en.wellness_tip: The strong and enduring body type benefits the most from Deodeok's ability to moisturize dry lungs and reduce coughing, phlegm, and asthma.
en.bio_synergy: Deodeok contains abundant isoflavones and saponins that directly stimulate the activity of immune cells in the blood vessels, significantly improving the activity of white blood cells.

en.name: Susam (Fresh Ginseng) Water Kimchi
en.description: This is a premium traditional Korean medicine soup broth made with fresh rehmannia, water dropwort, radish, garlic, ginger, and other ingredients. The ginseng aroma fills the mouth.
en.ingredients: 3 roots of rehmannia, 200g radish, 1 large cucumber, 1/2 small pear, 30g water dropwort, 5 cloves jujube, 3 tablespoons glutinous rice flour, 3 garlic cloves, 1/2 tablespoon ginger, 2 tablespoons salt, 6 cups of water
steps: Shred the rehmannia into thin strips and rinse it in water. Peel off the seeds from the jujube and cut it into small pieces.
Cut the radish, cucumber, and pear into thin slices and marinate them in salt for 15 minutes before draining.
Mix glutinous rice flour with saltwater to make a clear broth, then add minced garlic and ginger. Add the rehmannia, vegetables, jujube, and stir well.
Pour the prepared water kimchi broth into a pot containing the shredded rehmannia, marinated vegetables, jujube, and minced garlic. Simmer on low heat for 24 hours before cooling and refrigerating.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for Lesser Yin Type – Thoughtful & Sensitive Body Type
en.wellness_tip: The ginseng aroma in this soup is particularly beneficial for those with a lesser yin constitution, as it helps to improve digestion, strengthen immunity, and reduce fatigue.
en.bio_synergy: The bioactive saponins (spermidine) from the rehmannia enhances the permeability of gut microorganisms, increasing blood circulation in the capillaries.

en.name: Gochusip (Pepper Leaf) Kimchi
en.description: This spicy and tangy kimchi is made by fermenting the low-sugar leaves of garlic chives after harvesting them in late autumn. It is rich in vitamins, especially vitamin C, with a unique aroma.
en.ingredients: 500g of garlic chive leaves, 100g of radish sprouts, 5 tablespoons of ground chili flakes, 3 tablespoons of fermented anchovy paste, 1.5 tablespoons of finely chopped garlic, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 4 tablespoons of green onion juice, and 1/4 cup of sesame oil.
steps: Remove the woody part from the garlic chive leaves and soak them in hot salted water until they are soft. Drain and rinse with cold water to remove excess salt.
Boil the radish sprouts in warm salted water, then drain and cool slightly.

Mix together ground chili flakes, fermented anchovy paste, garlic, green onion juice, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Add this mixture to the softened garlic chive leaves and radish sprouts until well combined.
Add the mixed ingredients into a pot with enough water to cover them tightly, bring it to a boil, then simmer for 15-20 minutes until they are soft and tender.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for both Lesser Yin Type (Soy Sound) and Greater Yin Type (Strong Enduring) individuals.
en.wellness_tip: The spicy and tangy taste of the garlic chive leaves and radish sprouts helps support the weak lower body of a Lesser Yin type individual, while stimulating appetite in a Greater Yin type individual to enhance vitality.
en.bio_synergy: Rich in carotenoids and vitamin C, these ingredients stimulate beneficial probiotic cell production within the large intestine and help neutralize harmful active oxygen radicals.

en.name: Minari Kimchi (Water Dropwort Kimchi)
en.description: This springtime favorite, a light and cooling detox vegetable kimchi made with water dropwort, seasoned lightly with garlic, and served in the traditional manner.
en.ingredients: 500g of water dropwort, 1 medium onion, 3 tablespoons of minced garlic, 2 tablespoons of minced radish, 1 tablespoon of fermented soybean paste, 1 tablespoon of chopped green onions, 1 tablespoon of honey, 1 cup of kimchi juice
steps: Wash the water dropwort thoroughly in saltwater for 10 minutes and then rinse it clean. Cut the onion into thin slices.
Mix together soybean paste, minced garlic, radish, chopped green onions, and honey to make a light seasoning.
Add the water dropwort and onion to the seasoning mixture and mix well. Let it sit at room temperature for 1 hour before serving in a spicy manner.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Lesser Yang Type
en.wellness_tip: The cooling nature of water dropwort and its strong detoxifying effects help clear heat in the blood, which is common in individuals with lesser yang type.
en.bio_synergy: Quercetin (a flavonoid) from water dropwort helps regulate the immune receptors on the intestinal wall, contributing to the reduction of inflammation in the large intestine and aiding in the recovery of the intestinal lining.

en.name: Buchu Kimchi (Chive Kimchi)
en.description: A warm and invigorating kimchi made by combining the warmth of chives with a rich, savory seasoning. This kimchi has a spicy, dry taste that becomes even more flavorful as it ages.
en.ingredients: 1 kg Korean spring onion, 1 cup ground ginger powder, 1/2 cup fish sauce, 3 tablespoons fish broth, 2 tablespoons minced garlic, 2 tablespoons honey, 1/2 cup white radish
steps: Wash the chives by removing the root end dirt and thoroughly rinsing them.
Place the washed chives in a bowl with ground ginger powder for 10 minutes to slightly marinate.
Mix together ground ginger powder, fish sauce, minced garlic, honey, and white radish into a smooth seasoning paste.
Marinate the drained chives in the seasoning paste until they are evenly coated.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Lesser Yin Type
en.wellness_tip: This is the best tonic for women with deficient yang qi who experience cold limbs, lower abdomen discomfort, or uterine cold syndrome.
en.bio_synergy: The natural aryl propionic acid components in chives form a protective barrier against overgrowth of harmful bacteria such as E. coli in the large intestine.

en.name: Ueong Kimchi (Burdock Root Kimchi)
en.description: This unique health kimchi is made by slicing and peeling the burdock root into thin strips, then simmering it in salted water to remove some of its natural flavor. It is seasoned with a mixture of garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and other seasonings before being allowed to mature for several days at room temperature.
en.ingredients: Burdock Root 600g, Cabbage 30g, Garlic Paste 4 tablespoons, Fish Sauce 2 tablespoons, Fish Oil 1 tablespoon, Finely Chopped Garlic 1.5 tablespoons, Soybean Paste 3 tablespoons
steps: Wash the burdock root and peel it to remove its outer layer. Soak in salted water for a while until some of its natural flavor is removed.
Mix garlic paste, fish sauce, ginger, soybean paste, and other seasonings.
Pour seasoned mixture into simmered burdock root and mix well with gloves on to ensure even seasoning distribution.
Add cabbage and stir once more before leaving at room temperature for two days. Store in a cool place after fermentation.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for Yin Yang types
en.wellness_tip: This kimchi is particularly beneficial for individuals with weak kidney function, as it helps improve urination and regulate blood pressure.
en.bio_synergy: The unique iritine (Inulin) and prebiotic fibers in burdock root promote the growth of beneficial lactobacillus bacteria in the large intestine.

en.name: Dallae Kkwong-Manong Kimchi
en.description: This kimchi is made using the wild deer (Kkwong) and marmot (Manong) from Jeju Island. It is spicy and chewy, designed to help strengthen the stomach of those with lesser yin type constitution who have weakened digestion.
en.ingredients: Jeju wild deer 300g, radish 100g, garlic paste 3 tablespoons, minced anchovy 2 tablespoons, chopped green onion 2 tablespoons, garlic 1 tablespoon, lotus root 1 tablespoon
steps: Remove the roots and outer skin of the wild deer (Kkwong) and rinse it in water. Cut it into pieces about 4 cm long.
Cut the radish thinly and marinate it in water before steaming it.
Mix anchovy paste, garlic paste, chopped green onion, garlic, lotus root, and lotus root to make a seasoning paste.
Combine the seasoned paste with the prepared ingredients and mix well. Let it ferment for 1 day in an earthenware jar before serving.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for those with lesser yin type constitution who have weakened digestion
en.wellness_tip: The spicy nature of wild deer (Kkwong) helps to strengthen the stomach of those with lesser yin type constitution, while the warm and moist properties of marmot (Manong) help to clear stagnant qi in those with greater yin type constitution.
en.bio_synergy: A substance similar to allantoin, which is a derivative of amino acids, reduces inflammation markers on the intestinal barrier and enhances overall vitality throughout the body.

en.name: Lactobacillus plantarum
en.description: This is a dominant probiotic strain during the middle and later stages of kimchi fermentation, with strong resistance to pathogens in the stomach and intestinal mucosa. It has high survival rates in the gut due to its plant-derived nature.
en.ingredients: kimchi fermentation broth and plant-based lactose-producing environment
steps: Soak cabbage and vegetables in salt water at an appropriate acidity (2-3%) to inhibit lactic acid bacteria growth.
Lactococcus garicina produces lactic acid, lowering the pH to around 4.5 during early fermentation stages.
The strong Lactobacillus plantarum strain dominates in a slightly alkaline environment and adds a strong tangy flavor.
Fermentation is completed at pH 4.2 or higher, with the product being stored at low temperatures (0-2°C) to control activity.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for both Greater Yin Type and Lesser Yang Type individuals
en.wellness_tip: The strong survival ability of plant-derived probiotics helps cleanse harmful substances in the large intestine, while also alleviating heat sensitivity in the lesser yang type's stomach.
en.bio_synergy: It produces significant amounts of lactic acid and acetic acid in the colon, strengthening the intestinal barrier and directly defending against pathogenic enteric bacteria.

en.name: Bifidobacterium lactis
en.description: This is a key beneficial bacterium found in traditional fermented products and the large intestine environment, unlike ordinary bifidobacteria that are susceptible to oxygen, it has strong anaerobic and aerotactic properties contributing to health-promoting fermentation of probiotic beverages.
en.ingredients: fermented soybean mash and lactic acid bacteria
steps: mixes with kimchi culture and bifidobacterium cultures before inoculation at low temperatures.
adds small amounts of prebiotics such as oligofructose to promote organic acid production.
cultivates anaerobically in a fermentative environment at 35-38°C for 24 hours without oxygen exposure.
ensures preservation of lactose and short-chain fatty acids by rapid refrigeration after vigorous cooling.
en.sasang_relation: beneficial to those with lesser Yin type
en.wellness_tip: strengthening the vitality of the digestive system, which is weakened in individuals with chronic constipation and diarrhea, it enhances the large intestine's immune response and keeps the stomach warm.
en.bio_synergy: stabilizes the epithelial cells lining the large intestine, activating the production of immunoglobulin A (IgA) and preventing viral gastroenteritis.

en.name: Pediococcus pentosaceus
en.description: A potent probiotic isolated from fermented foods with high traditional lactic acid content, which produces bacteriocin Pediocin to kill harmful foodborne pathogens.
en.ingredients: Traditional yogurt and whey fermentation medium with high salt concentration
steps: Mix the fish with a high concentration of starter culture for 20% or more to prevent pathogenic bacteria from originating.
Maintain an appropriate temperature to incubate Pediococcus strain.
Helps break down plant and animal organic matter into peptides by promoting protease activity in microorganisms.
Ensures that sufficient amounts of bacteriocin Pediocin are accumulated for 6 months or more under low-temperature, high-salt conditions.
en.sasang_relation: Recommended for Yin/Yang types
en.wellness_tip: Helps neutralize harmful amines produced when consuming pickled or fermented foods to benefit Yin and Yang individuals.
en.bio_synergy: Protects against foodborne pathogens by breaking down the protective barrier of Clostridium, Listeria, and Streptococcus species that thrive in low-temperature, high-salt environments.

en.name: Lactobacillus gasseri
en.description: A probiotic strain originating from Korean mothers' milk and fermented grains, it is a traditional hybrid beneficial bacteria that strengthens the intestinal barrier immunity.
en.ingredients: Plant-derived organic polysaccharides and mother's milk culture medium
steps: Soak in cereal complex carbohydrates to create a sterile fermentation vessel.
Sprinkle Lactobacillus gasseri strain at a certain ratio.
Grow under an environment similar to body temperature, 36.5°C, for 18 hours without oxygen.
When the lactose concentration reaches the optimal level, perform low-temperature sterilization and seal.
en.sasang_relation: Most effective for those with Greater Yin Type
en.wellness_tip: It significantly contributes to weight loss in individuals prone to obesity due to reduced fat metabolism efficiency.
en.bio_synergy: Blocks the absorption of fatty acids and induces their excretion through the digestive tract, strongly stimulating the inhibition pathway of white adipose tissue in the intestinal microflora.

en.name: Three-Cheop Banseum (Basic Table Setting)
en.description: This is a traditional Korean meal presentation that was commonly used by common people in the Joseon Dynasty. It consists of rice, porridge, and soybean sprouts as the main dishes, along with three side dishes to harmonize with them.
en.ingredients: Brown rice (or millet porridge), soybean soup (soybean sprout soup), napa cabbage kimchi, soy sauce, fermented bean paste stewed meat, pickled vegetables, grilled fish, stir-fried vegetables
steps: Place the basic ingredients of brown rice and porridge in the rice bowl and pot respectively. Arrange them on the left side of the table.
Arrange the basic seasonings such as soy sauce and fermented bean paste stewed meat at the center of the table, where both hands can easily reach them.
Stir-fried vegetables and pickled vegetables are placed on the right side of the rice and porridge in a stable position.
Place pickled vegetables (kimchi), grilled fish, stir-fried vegetables, etc., in neat rows to balance the dish.
en.sasang_relation: Suitable for all constitutions (normal)
en.wellness_tip: This meal is designed to help people with digestive issues such as lesser Yin types and greater Yang types. It provides a gentle way to improve digestion.
en.bio_synergy: The fermentation of soybean sprouts (soy sauce) and kimchi rich in micro-nutrients stimulate the production of short-chain fatty acids (SCFA) in the large intestine, which is beneficial for maintaining gut health.

en.name: Five-Cheop Banseum (Standard Table Setting)
en.description: A traditional table setting for middle-class families, or as a light offering to guests of lower status, this is an ideal standard Korean meal set that balances nutrition and taste.
en.ingredients: Brown rice porridge, minced pork, Napa cabbage kimchi, pickled radish, soy sauce, fermented bean paste, two types of vegetables, fish cake, boiled eggs (or omelette)
steps: Place a warm bowl of brown rice porridge and cold radish kimchi on the left side of the table to warm up the stomach.
Arrange basic dishes such as soy sauce and broth in a horizontal line at the center of the table.
Serve steamed or fried foods immediately after cooking on the right side of the table.
Place pickled vegetables and vegetable soups, cold side dishes (e.g., eel stir-fry), and boiled eggs (or omelette) on the left side to balance temperature and harmonize flavors.
en.sasang_relation: Recommend for Yin/Yang types
en.wellness_tip: This set is recommended for those with weak bones and blood vessels, as it contains calcium-rich eel and high-protein eggs that help maintain healthy digestion.
en.bio_synergy: The complete combination of animal protein from meat sources and plant-based dietary fiber from vegetables helps improve the efficiency of digestive system absorption while preventing excessive insulin secretion.

en.name: 7-Cheop Bansang (Noble Table Setting)
en.description: This is a high-grade table setting for prominent families of the Joseon Dynasty, featuring additional dishes such as soups and porridge. It emphasizes advanced cooking techniques and optimal balance between meat and vegetables in terms of nutrition.
ingredients: kimchi stew with green onions and chili oil
steps: Arrange two types of rice (sourdough rice, millet porridge) in front to expand the selection range for dishes.
Place a side dish with hot soup and cold food alternately to create visual rhythm.

Place braised pork ribs with abundant fat on one side of the main dish center to enhance the main flavor, and place steamed green onions and chili oil stewed in soy sauce on the other side to capture the refreshing taste.
Arrange seven types of condiments such as pickled vegetables, fish cake, and fish cake in a round shape at the back of the rice bowl.
en.sasang_relation: Beneficial for both Lesser Yang Type (Sour) and Greater Yin Type (Strong)
en.wellness_tip: The abundant amino acids in braised pork ribs benefit the functions of the greater yang type, while the refreshing soybean paste helps cool down the heat-sensitive lesser yang type.
en.bio_synergy: The combination of traditional fermented vegetables and radish condiments activates the action of major organs, inhibiting inflammatory reactions caused by saturated fatty acid-induced oxidation in the stomach lining.

en.name: 9-Cheop Bansang (Deluxe Feast Setting)
en.description: This traditional Korean food setting was used for special occasions such as weddings, funerals, and grand banquets where important guests were invited. It is a complete meal featuring the essence of traditional Korean cuisine.
steps: Place the fresh pork belly and vegetables in a steamer pot with clean water. Steam until cooked.
Arrange the eel roe and salmon roe on either side of the pork ribs.
Place the fresh seafood and day's catch dishes on the right side of the meal.
Place minty green onion salad and pear chestnuts on the left side to stimulate taste buds appropriately.
en.sasang_relation: Best for Lesser Yin Type (Soojung) and Greater Yin Type (Taejung) individuals
en.wellness_tip: The warm brown rice porridge, pork ribs, and eel's qi help strengthen the vitality of Lesser Yin Type individuals. The taurine in salmon roe and eel broth helps detoxify the liver of Greater Yin Type individuals.
en.bio_synergy: A balanced combination of abundant seafood (eel, salmon) and high-quality protein (pork ribs, eel) promotes cellular communication and strengthens blood vessel wall elasticity.

en.name: 12-Cheop Surasang (Royal King's Table)
en.description: This is the pinnacle of Korean food culture, a traditional table setting that has been served to kings and queens in the royal court. It consists of 12 carefully selected premium condiments including high-quality soups such as miso soup, clear stew, and rice dishes.
ingredients: mixed vegetables, including green onions and red peppers
salted vegetables, including kimchi and radish sprouts
steps: Place two types of rice, 'white glutinous rice' and 'red glutinous rice', in a proper manner on the left side of the king's seat.
Arrange the most premium nourishing pork stew (sour meat) at the center of the table. Place clear broth stew and braised pork stew to both sides.
Place various dishes such as kimchi, radish sprouts, and rice cakes in a proper manner on the right side of the table.
